scenario: Draft a plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000 given as a hand loan. The plaintiff Ram Kumar, aged 45, resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, advanced Rs.15,00,000 to the defendant Suresh Patel, aged 38, resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, on 15.03.2024 via NEFT bank transfer (UTR: AXIB20240315123456). There was no written agreement. The defendant acknowledged the loan via WhatsApp messages. Despite repeated oral demands and a legal notice dated 01.07.2024, the defendant has failed to repay. The plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum from the date of default (20.06.2024). File in City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

scorecard.detail: At least one statutory section cited

draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE CITY CIVIL COURT
AT BENGALURU

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

Ram Kumar,
Aged about 45 years,
Residing at: Jayanagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Suresh Patel,
Aged about 38 years,
Residing at: Koramangala, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF MONEY LENT / ADVANCE PAID WITH INTEREST AND COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute the present suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued.

2. This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to try and entertain this suit inasmuch as the cause of action arose within the local limits of the jurisdiction of this Court at Bengaluru, and the Defendant resides within such territorial limits.

3. This Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try this suit, the suit being valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Court.

LIMITATION

4. The present suit is filed within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 19 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, as the suit is for money lent and the cause of action arose on 20.06.2024 when the Defendant committed default.

FACTS OF THE TRANSACTION

5. On 15.03.2024, the Plaintiff advanced a sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- to the Defendant as a hand loan.

6. The said amount was transferred by the Plaintiff to the Defendant's bank account through NEFT bearing UTR No. AXIB20240315123456, as evidenced by the bank record marked as Annexure A.

7. There was no written loan agreement executed between the parties regarding the advance. The Defendant acknowledged the receipt of the said loan amount through WhatsApp messages exchanged with the Plaintiff, copies of which are marked as Annexure B.

8. The loan was advanced on the understanding that the Defendant would repay the same on demand.

DEFAULT AND DEMAND

9. The Plaintiff made repeated oral demands upon the Defendant for repayment of the loan amount.

10. The Defendant failed to repay the amount despite such demands and committed default on 20.06.2024.

11. Consequently, the Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 to the Defendant calling upon him to repay the outstanding dues, as per the copy marked as Annexure C.

12. The Defendant has failed to respond to the said legal notice and has not repaid the loan amount or any part thereof till date.

QUANTIFICATION OF CLAIM

13. The principal amount due and payable by the Defendant to the Plaintiff is Rs. 15,00,000/-.

14. The Plaintiff is entitled to interest on the said principal amount at the rate of 12% per annum from 20.06.2024, being the date of default, till date of realisation.

15. The interest claimed is simple interest and not compound interest, as there is no agreement between the parties for payment of compound interest.

16. The Defendant has not made any part-payment towards the loan amount. The total outstanding comprises the principal sum and accrued interest calculated at the aforementioned rate.

LEGAL BASIS

17. The Defendant received a sum of Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}}/- from the Plaintiff and is liable to repay the same with interest under Section 70 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, as the Defendant has enjoyed the benefit of the non-gratuitous advance made by the Plaintiff.

18. The present suit is maintainable under Section 9 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

CAUSE OF ACTION

19. The cause of action for this suit first arose on 20.06.2024 when the Defendant committed default in repaying the loan amount despite demand, giving rise to the Plaintiff's right to institute this suit.

20. The cause of action continues to subsist as the Defendant has failed to comply with the legal notice dated 01.07.2024 and has not repaid the outstanding dues.

21. The breach was a single event. The Plaintiff's loss and right to claim damages continue until compensated in accordance with law.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

22. The suit is valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. Court fee on the plaint has been computed at Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}/- under the provisions of the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958 as applicable in the State of Karnataka, with reference to {{COURT_FEE_COMPUTATION_BASIS}} and the applicable rate schedule. The plaint is tendered with the requisite court fee.

INTEREST

23. The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount as follows:

    (a) Pendente lite interest at such rate as the Court deems fit and just from the date of institution of the suit until the date of decree, under Order XX Rule 11 CPC;
    (b) Future interest at such rate as the Court deems fit and just from the date of decree until realization of the entire decretal amount.

PRAYER

In the pr
